name: Transit
content: The Blue Line will take you from the airport to downtown. That is about all I know when it comes to transit. The rest of Minneapolis is very bikeable. I have not been there in the winter, though from what I can gather, people just wear thick gloves and ski helmets when biking in the winter. There are several dedicated bike corridors that run throughout the city, reducing the number of times you have to stop and restart at intersections. There is also a public car-share service, where you can pick up and drop off a car anywhere in the city for a fee. Completing service tasks (like re-filling or recharging the car) will give you free mileage. Pretty nifty, I wish more cities had something like this.
